title: ImageObservation
description: Structured visual analysis of a single moodboard image. Schema mirrors docs/vision/architecture.md §4.1. All claim fields are nullable to tolerate model uncertainty — missing fields are normal, but generic vibe summaries (e.g. 'green soft UI') are explicitly rejected via the prompt.

source.description: Identifying information for the analyzed image. Filled in by the loader, not the model — but the model echoes source.id back so downstream tools can correlate.

id.description: Stable id assigned at load time, e.g. 'img_01'.

styleLabels.description: Specific labels like 'botanical', '3d-diorama', 'frosted-glass'. NOT vague mood labels.

materials.description: Identified surface materials with evidence. THIS is where the 'real / tactile / raised' signal lives — keep this section rich and specific.

label.description: Specific material name, e.g. 'frosted glass panel', 'matte clay 3d object'. Not generic adjectives.

evidence.description: What in the image supports this label.

shadowType.description: Free-form. Multiple types allowed in one phrase, e.g. 'large soft cast + contact shadow'.

depth.description: Layering, overlap, boundary-breaking. Critical for 'diorama / layered scene' vs 'flat UI' distinction.

overlapPattern.description: e.g. 'plants break the card boundary', 'strictly contained'.

layoutType.description: e.g. 'central object panel with organic frame'.

edgeBehavior.description: e.g. 'foreground crosses container boundaries' vs 'strictly contained'.

components.description: Visual UI/component prototypes (not literal DOM elements — 'hero-card', 'glass-panel', 'floating-chip').

implementationHint.description: How to actually build it in CSS. Most useful when specific.

headingStyle.description: Style inference, not font identification. e.g. 'geometric sans, soft, low-contrast'.

imageryStyle.description: What KIND of imagery this is — photography vs 3D vs clay vs cutout vs screenshot. Distinct from material (which is about surfaces inside the image).

implementationHints.description: Hard-won, specific CSS/build rules. NOT generic ('use rounded corners') — specific ('use ::before for warm glow + absolute foreground layer for plants breaking boundary').

antiPatterns.description: What this kind of imagery is often miscopied as. SCOPE: this image's own anti-patterns, NOT what other moodboard images forbid (that's synthesis-stage).
